data.content:
The command to run tests only on `net9.0` is:

```
dotnet test --framework net9.0
```

This successfully built and targeted only `net9.0`. The "no test available" message is expected — your project doesn't have any test source files (`.cs` files with test classes) yet. Once you add test classes, this command will discover and run them for `net9.0` only.
data.reasoningText:
There are no test source files. The project has test packages but no test classes. That's why no tests are discovered. But the user's question is just about how to run tests on net9.0 only — the command itself is correct.

The command to run tests only on net9.0 is:

```
dotnet test --framework net9.0
```

This worked (it built and ran for net9.0 only). The "no test available" message is because there are no test classes in the project, not because of the framework flag.
